data.title: Denison Mines Secures Peter Ballantyne Cree Nation Support for Wheeler River Project
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Denison Mines ( (TSE:DML) ) has shared an update.

On July 2, 2026, Denison Mines reported that Peter Ballantyne Cree Nation had withdrawn its judicial review application challenging Saskatchewan’s environmental approval for the Phoenix In-Situ Recovery uranium mine. Following further engagement between the parties, PBCN has formally given its consent to and support for the development and operation of Denison’s Wheeler River Project in northern Saskatchewan.

The resolution removes a key legal and social-risk overhang for Wheeler River, the largest undeveloped uranium project in the eastern Athabasca Basin, and reinforces Denison’s social licence to advance Phoenix and Gryphon. With provincial and federal environmental assessments approved and a construction licence in place, Denison is proceeding with site preparation and early works at Phoenix, underpinning its plan to bring one of the world’s lowest-cost uranium mining operations into production by 2028.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:DML) stock is a Buy
with a C$6.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Denison Mines stock,
see the TSE:DML Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DML Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DML is a Neutral.

The score is primarily weighed down by weak financial performance (large TTM losses, persistent cash burn, and materially higher leverage). Technical indicators add a modestly negative tilt due to price trading below key short-/mid-term averages and slightly negative momentum. Valuation provides limited support given the negative P/E and no stated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on DML stock,
click here.

More about Denison Mines

Denison Mines Corp. is a uranium exploration and development company focused on Canada’s Athabasca Basin, where it holds a dominant portfolio of properties and joint venture interests. Its flagship asset is the Wheeler River Project, in which it has an effective 95% interest and acts as operator, alongside stakes in the McClean Lake and Midwest joint ventures and other nearby deposits that support its long-term uranium production strategy.

The company also owns 50% of JCU (Canada) Exploration Company Limited, gaining exposure to additional uranium projects including Millennium, Kiggavik and Christie Lake. Denison, which marked its 70th year in uranium mining, exploration and development in 2024, leverages existing regional infrastructure such as the McClean Lake mill and has begun site preparation and early construction at the high-grade Phoenix In-Situ Recovery uranium mine, targeting first production in 2028.

Average Trading Volume: 3,457,772

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$3.94B

Learn more about DML stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: KEPCO Details Governance Structure and ESG Oversight in 2026 Report
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Korea Electric Power ( (KEP) ) has provided an update.

KEPCO has released an English summary of its Corporate Governance Report, detailing the governance framework in place as of May 31, 2026 and aimed at improving investor understanding of its oversight systems. The company reports a governance compliance rate of 73.3% for 2025, noting partial adoption of best practices such as electronic voting and independent internal audit structures, while still lacking measures like extended AGM notice and predictable dividend policies.

The report emphasizes a board structure where eight non-standing directors outnumber seven standing directors, with the chair drawn from non-standing members to reinforce independent decision-making and transparent oversight. KEPCO also highlights four specialized board subcommittees, including an Audit Committee and an ESG Committee first established in December 2020 among Korean public enterprises, which together strengthen risk management, sustainable business strategy and director nominations, thereby shaping the company’s governance and accountability to shareholders and stakeholders.

Spark’s Take on KEP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KEP is a Neutral.

The score is supported by very attractive valuation (low P/E and solid dividend yield) and improved profitability/cash generation. These positives are tempered by a leveraged balance sheet and weak technical trend/momentum despite oversold signals.

To see Spark’s full report on KEP stock,
click here.

More about Korea Electric Power

Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) is South Korea’s dominant state-influenced utility, responsible for electricity generation, transmission and distribution across the country. Listed on domestic and international exchanges, it operates in the energy and power sector with a focus on large-scale grid infrastructure and serving both industrial and retail electricity consumers.

data.title: Integrated BioPharma Updates and Extends Credit Facilities
data.description:

Integrated BioPharma ( (INBP) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On June 30, 2026, Integrated BioPharma and Manhattan Drug Company amended their loan arrangements with PNC Bank, extending the expiration of their existing committed revolving line of credit to April 14, 2027 while maintaining the $4,000,000 borrowing limit. The amendment also introduced a new $250,000 Convertible Line of Credit Note dedicated to equipment and vehicle purchases, replacing a matured $500,000 Convertible Equipment Line of Credit and adjusting interest terms tied to daily one-month SOFR plus 250 basis points.

These changes refined the company’s capital structure by rolling over its equipment-focused borrowing facility into a smaller, convertible line of credit with flexible interest options after a defined conversion date. The revised credit profile is likely to influence how Integrated BioPharma finances asset acquisitions and manages liquidity, with potential implications for its cost of capital and operational investment planning.

data.title: Centrus Energy Secures Major HALEU Enrichment Contract
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

High conviction LEU bulls now have this Tradr ETF

Centrus Energy ( (LEU) ) just unveiled an update.

On June 30, 2026, Centrus subsidiary American Centrifuge Operating, LLC signed a $900 million fixed-price contract with the U.S. Department of Energy to establish new commercial HALEU enrichment capacity at its Piketon, Ohio facility. The agreement requires deployment of specified enrichment capacity and delivery of one metric ton of HALEU UF6 by March 2032, with DOE options for up to an additional 10 metric tons valued at $170 million, bringing potential total contract value to about $1.07 billion.

The company announced on July 1, 2026 that it has completed production of the final 900 kilograms of HALEU UF6 under its prior demonstration contract, achieving more than 1,900 kilograms cumulatively and finishing ahead of schedule. Centrus is now transitioning from demonstration to commercialization, planning initial build-out of 12 metric tons of annual HALEU capacity alongside LEU output to serve a $2.4 billion LEU backlog, a multi-billion-dollar expansion expected to support thousands of jobs in Ohio, Tennessee and across its U.S. supply chain.

The most recent analyst rating on (LEU) stock is a Buy
with a $285.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Centrus Energy stock,
see the LEU Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LEU Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LEU is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weakened cash flow (negative TTM OCF/FCF), margin compression, and a bearish price trend below key moving averages, with a high P/E adding valuation risk. Offsetting these are constructive earnings-call signals (raised revenue guidance, sizable backlog, DOE award, and strong reported liquidity) and positive corporate developments supporting long-term HALEU expansion.

To see Spark’s full report on LEU stock,
click here.

More about Centrus Energy

Centrus Energy Corp. is a U.S.-based supplier of nuclear fuel and related services to the nuclear power industry, focused on providing clean, carbon-free energy solutions. The company is pioneering production of High-Assay, Low-Enriched Uranium (HALEU) and expanding Low-Enriched Uranium (LEU) capacity, aiming to restore large-scale domestic uranium enrichment capabilities for energy, security and national security needs.

Since 1998, Centrus has supplied more than 1,850 reactor years of fuel to utility customers, equivalent to over 7 billion tons of coal displaced. Its expansion program centers on advanced centrifuge technology, with major operations in Piketon, Ohio, and a centrifuge manufacturing plant in Oak Ridge, Tennessee, supported by a nationwide supplier network.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $3.27B

data.title: SOL Strategies Tightens Solana Focus With Houdini Swap Integration and Debt Reduction
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest announcement is out from Sol Strategies ( (TSE:HODL) ).

In its June 2026 business update released on July 2, 2026, SOL Strategies reported the closing of its Houdini Swap acquisition on June 1 and a key integration with multi‑chain app Jumper on June 18. The integration makes private swap, bridge and receive transactions available directly on Jumper, extending Houdini Swap’s reach into a partner network that collectively claims tens of millions of registered users and processes over $1 billion in monthly volume.

The company also detailed balance‑sheet actions on June 8, settling about CAD $5.75 million of debt via the sale of 65,001 SOL, leaving 460,017 SOL in treasury, worth roughly CAD $50.6 million as of June 30. During June, SOL Strategies wound down non‑Solana validators on the Sui and Monad networks to sharpen its focus on Solana infrastructure and privacy technology, after its validator network delivered 100% uptime and net 661 SOL earned, while STKESOL reached 646,528 SOL staked.

Houdini Swap completed its first full month under SOL Strategies with cumulative swap volume of more than USD $2.7 billion and over 1.1 million swaps since launch, including over USD $65 million and 34,000 swaps in June alone. Management said these developments mark a transition from a pure validator operation to an integrated platform spanning validators, liquid staking, institutional staking and privacy infrastructure, leaving the company in a materially different strategic position heading into the final quarter of its financial year.

The update also signaled a communications shift, as SOL Strategies will discontinue monthly business updates in favor of event‑driven releases, quarterly earnings and ad‑hoc announcements. The company highlighted its participation in the H.C. Wainwright 28th Annual Global Investment Conference in New York in mid‑September 2026, underscoring an ongoing effort to engage investors as it refines its Solana‑focused growth strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:HODL) stock is a Buy
with a C$29.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sol Strategies stock,
see the TSE:HODL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HODL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HODL is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (heavy losses, negative operating/free cash flow, and rising leverage) and bearish technicals (price below key moving averages with negative MACD). The latest earnings call adds partial support through credible growth initiatives (STKESOL traction and the expected revenue contribution from Houdini), but near-term liquidity and crypto-volatility/accounting headwinds remain significant. Valuation provides limited support given losses (negative P/E) and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on HODL stock,
click here.

More about Sol Strategies

SOL Strategies Inc. is a Toronto‑based digital asset infrastructure company focused on high‑performance blockchain and privacy technologies. It operates Solana‑based staking infrastructure, a liquid staking token and privacy tools, serving individual SOL holders and institutional clients across public blockchain networks.

The company’s portfolio includes proprietary Solana validators, the STKESOL liquid staking platform and the Houdini Swap cross‑chain privacy aggregator. With listings on the CSE under HODL and NASDAQ under STKE, SOL Strategies positions itself as a specialist in Solana‑centric infrastructure and privacy‑enhancing services for a broad market of crypto participants.

TAP Real Estate ate Technologies ( (RWAX) ) has provided an update.

On December 30, 2025, TAP Real Estate Technologies, Inc. entered into a license agreement with TAP, Inc. to use TAP’s token engine, blockchain registry, wallet, and related technology in the real estate sector. That original agreement was due to expire on June 30, 2026.

On June 29, 2026, the parties replaced it with a perpetual, amended and restated license that grants TAP Real Estate Technologies exclusive rights against third parties in real estate, while TAP retains its own usage rights. The company agreed to pay a total of $700,000 for the royalty-free license, of which $695,000 has already been paid and the remaining $5,000 is due by September 30, 2026, making the license fully paid up upon final payment.

TAP Real Estate Technologies, Inc. operates in the real estate technology sector, focusing on tokenization, blockchain-based registries, digital wallets, and related infrastructure tailored to property markets. data.description:

  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Obsidian Energy ( (TSE:OBE) ) has provided an announcement.

Obsidian Energy announced on July 2, 2026 that it closed its previously disclosed acquisition of Belly River light oil assets in the Wilson Creek area of Willesden Green on June 30, 2026, paying about $98 million and adding roughly 2,500 boe/d and 35 net sections of land. The deal, funded through its credit facilities, expands the scale of its Willesden Green position and is aimed at driving sustained production growth toward about 20,000 boe/d and higher free cash flow.

Following the acquisition, the company raised its 2026 average production guidance to 29,000–31,000 boe/d while keeping capital spending at $300–$325 million, and outlined a six-well program on the acquired lands in early 2027 to lift output from those assets to about 3,000 boe/d. Management now targets approximately 22% total production growth and 28% light oil growth in 2027, expecting lower operating and break-even costs and highlighting a stronger balance sheet and expanded credit facility as key support for its shareholder-focused growth plans.

Itau Unibanco ( (ITUB) ) has shared an update.

On July 1, 2026, Itaú Unibanco announced that it has won a new bidding process run by the Government of the State of Minas Gerais to continue providing payment services for roughly 670,000 state employees, retirees, pensioners, and corporate suppliers. The newly executed agreement covers a five-year term starting December 22, 2026, ensuring continuity of Itaú’s role in handling the state’s payroll and reinforcing its footprint in Brazil’s public-sector financial services.

Under the new contract, Itaú Unibanco S.A., a subsidiary of the holding company, will pay a total of BRL 2.188 billion for the payroll management rights, an amount that will be booked as an intangible asset and recognized in deferred profit or loss. The deal secures a large, stable client base for the bank, potentially supporting long-term revenue streams while impacting its balance sheet through the sizable intangible asset, a development that is relevant for investors monitoring Itaú’s capital allocation and public-sector exposure.

The most recent analyst rating on (ITUB) stock is a Buy
with a $9.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Itau Unibanco stock,

Itaú Unibanco Holding S.A. is Brazil’s largest private-sector financial institution, offering a broad range of banking and payment services to individuals, companies, and public-sector clients. The bank has a significant presence in government-related financial operations, including payroll management and payment services for state employees and suppliers, reinforcing its role in the country’s financial infrastructure.

The company already manages payroll and payment services for the Government of the State of Minas Gerais under a contract signed in July 2021, which remains effective through the end of 2026. This positioning underscores Itaú Unibanco’s focus on long-term public-sector relationships and large-scale transaction services in one of Brazil’s most populous states.

data.title: Pembina Pipeline Greenlights $4.6 Billion Alberta Gas Power Plant for Major Data Centre
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Pembina Pipeline ( (TSE:PPL) ) has issued an update.

Pembina Pipeline Corporation, together with Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners and Kineticor Asset Management, has approved a positive final investment decision for the Greenlight Electricity Centre, a 932 MW gas-fired combined cycle plant in Sturgeon County, Alberta, announced on July 2, 2026. The facility will supply dedicated, behind-the-meter power to a major data centre in Alberta’s Industrial Heartland, positioning Pembina as an early mover in serving the growing AI and cloud computing-driven data centre market.

Under a long-term tolling agreement, Greenlight will receive capacity and usage-based payments from the data centre customer, aligning the project with Pembina’s fee-based midstream model and providing a new stream of low-risk, contracted cash flows. The project, expected in service in the second half of 2030, has an estimated total cost of about $4.6 billion, or $2.3 billion net to Pembina, and is forecast to generate roughly $310 million in annual run-rate adjusted EBITDA, net to Pembina, supporting its 5–7 percent fee-based EBITDA per share growth target to 2030.

Pembina’s net investment in the project is expected to be approximately $2.1 billion after proceeds from a land sale to the data centre customer, with about 85 percent of capital costs secured under fixed-price agreements, including major equipment and services from Siemens Energy. The plant will require about 150 million cubic feet per day of natural gas, and Greenlight has already secured long-term gas transportation capacity on regional systems, enhancing redundancy and operational flexibility.

With ownership split between Pembina and MSIP at 47.5 percent each and Kineticor at 5 percent, the partners see GLEC as a scalable platform for future power-to-data centre projects, including potential expansion to 1,864 MW and further phases. The project has received all major regulatory approvals, and provincial and local leaders have framed it as a cornerstone investment for Alberta’s emerging data centre industry, job creation and broader economic growth, reinforcing Pembina’s strategic role in powering the province’s digital economy.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:PPL) stock is a Buy
with a C$69.00 price target.

More about Pembina Pipeline

Pembina Pipeline Corporation is a Canadian midstream energy company that transports, processes and markets natural gas and natural gas liquids across Western Canada and North America. The company focuses on fee-based infrastructure and is increasingly targeting adjacent growth areas, such as gas-fired power for large industrial and data centre customers in Alberta’s Industrial Heartland.

Pembina leverages long-term, contracted models to generate stable cash flows and supports Canadian natural gas production through processing, transportation and related services. Its emerging gas-to-power platform is designed to catalyze new intra-basin gas demand while diversifying its revenue base beyond traditional oil and gas shippers.

The company often partners with institutional investors and specialized developers to co-own large assets, and applies its construction and project management expertise to deliver complex energy infrastructure on time and on budget.

data.title: Cameco Temporarily Halts Cigar Lake Mining After McClean Lake Mill Shutdown
data.description:

Cameco ( (TSE:CCO) ) has provided an announcement.

On July 1, 2026 Cameco announced a temporary suspension of mining at its Cigar Lake uranium operation in northern Saskatchewan, following operational problems at Orano’s McClean Lake mill, where Cigar Lake ore is processed. The mill’s sulfuric acid plant was shut down for repairs, and with limited ore storage at the mine, Cameco halted extraction until sufficient acid supply enables milling to resume.

Cameco expects the McClean Lake mill to restart in roughly two weeks and currently does not foresee an impact on its 2026 production outlook for Cigar Lake, though it acknowledges the risk that extended repairs or difficulties sourcing alternative sulfuric acid could alter that assessment. The brief disruption underscores operational interdependence within the uranium fuel supply chain, but Cameco’s guidance suggests limited near-term consequences for production and customer commitments if the issue is resolved on the anticipated timeline.

data.title: Agnico Eagle Halts Barnat Pit Mining After Wall Movement, Trims 2026–2028 Output Outlook
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Agnico Eagle ( (TSE:AEM) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 1, 2026, Agnico Eagle reported a rock mass movement along the north wall of the Barnat open pit at the Canadian Malartic complex in Quebec, prompting a precautionary suspension of mining in the pit. There were no injuries, equipment damage or environmental impacts, and the event occurred in a zone already under enhanced geotechnical monitoring and safety exclusion protocols.

During the halt in pit mining, the Canadian Malartic processing plant will run on low-grade stockpiled ore to limit near-term disruption, but the company still expects a production loss of 60,000 to 80,000 ounces of gold at the site in the second half of 2026. As a result, full-year 2026 output is now projected near the lower end of the 3.3 million to 3.5 million ounce guidance range, and Barnat-related constraints are currently seen reducing annual production by up to 150,000 ounces in both 2027 and 2028.

Agnico Eagle’s technical teams are conducting a detailed geotechnical assessment to determine when operations at Barnat can restart safely, and further updates to production and cost guidance are due with second-quarter results on July 29, 2026. The company says the incident does not affect development or the production outlook for the Odyssey mine, nor its longer-term goal of reaching 1 million ounces of annual gold output from the Canadian Malartic complex in the early 2030s.

VisionWave Holdings ( (VWAV) ) has issued an update.

On July 1, 2026, VisionWave Holdings entered into a Distributor Agreement with Stratonex Defence Technologies, appointing the UK-based firm as its strategic commercialization, integration and sovereign delivery partner for the United Kingdom, Europe and other agreed markets. The framework tasks Stratonex with identifying and managing government, defense and institutional opportunities for VisionWave’s AI-powered defense, security and autonomous systems, while supporting systems integration and sovereign deployment.

The agreement introduces an opportunity registration process that grants Stratonex exclusive protection on VisionWave-approved opportunities during the registration period, but it does not confer broad exclusivity, minimum purchase obligations or revenue commitments, and future sales will depend on individual customer contracts. VisionWave’s board approved the deal after reviewing Stratonex co-founder Ben Everitt’s existing role as an independent advisory board member, with the company emphasizing the partnership as a milestone in its international growth strategy amid ongoing UK and European investment in defense modernization.

More about VisionWave Holdings

data.title: RenovoRx Granted Extended Nasdaq Compliance Period Amid Listing Risk
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest announcement is out from RenovoRx ( (RNXT) ).

On December 31, 2025, RenovoRx received a deficiency notice from Nasdaq stating that its common stock had fallen below the $1.00 minimum bid price required for continued listing on The Nasdaq Capital Market. The company was initially given until June 30, 2026 to regain compliance under the applicable Nasdaq rules.

On July 1, 2026, RenovoRx was notified that Nasdaq had granted a second 180-day compliance period, extending the deadline to December 28, 2026 for the stock to trade at or above $1.00 for at least 10 consecutive business days. Failure to meet this threshold by the new deadline would trigger a delisting notice, though the company would retain the right to appeal to a Nasdaq Hearings Panel, underscoring ongoing listing risk for shareholders.

with a $14.00 price target.

TJGC Group Limited ( (TJGC) ) has issued an announcement.

On July 1, 2026, TJGC Group Limited received confirmation from Nasdaq’s Listings Qualifications Department that it had regained compliance with the exchange’s minimum bid price requirement. Nasdaq determined that between June 16 and June 30, 2026, TJGC’s ordinary shares closed at or above $1.00 for at least 10 consecutive business days, bringing the earlier deficiency notice issued on March 26, 2026 to a close.

Regaining compliance removes the immediate risk of delisting tied to the minimum bid price rule and stabilizes TJGC’s status on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The resolution of the issue helps preserve access to U.S. capital markets and may reassure shareholders and other stakeholders about the company’s adherence to key listing standards.

More about TJGC Group Limited
